lines: +        'subtitle' => 'The DocumentsDB service allows you to create structured collections of documents, query and filter lists of documents.',
+        'subtitle' => 'The TablesDB service allows you to create structured tables of columns, query and filter lists of rows.',
+        'subtitle' => 'The VectorsDB service allows you to create vector collections, embeddings, indexes, and documents.',
+        'subtitle' => 'The Webhooks service allows you to configure event-driven HTTP callbacks for your project.',
+        'subtitle' => 'The Tokens service allows you to create and manage resource tokens for project resources.',
+        'subtitle' => 'The Presences service allows you to create, query, and manage realtime presence state.',
+        'subtitle' => 'The Assistant service allows Console users to ask assistant queries.',
+        'subtitle' => 'The Organization service allows you to manage organization-level projects.',

->param('host', '', new Text(0), 'SMTP hosts. Either a single hostname or multiple semicolon-delimited hostnames. You can also specify a different port for each host such as `smtp1.example.com:25;smtp2.example.com`. You can also specify encryption type, for example: `tls://smtp1.example.com:587;ssl://smtp2.example.com:465"`. Hosts will be tried in order.')
->param('port', 587, new Range(1, 65535), 'The default SMTP server port.', true)
->param('encryption', '', new WhiteList(['none', 'ssl', 'tls']), 'Encryption type. Can be omitted, \'ssl\', or \'tls\'', true, enum: new Enum(name: 'SmtpEncryption'))
->param('autoTLS', true, new Boolean(), 'Enable SMTP AutoTLS feature.', true)
->param('mailer', '', new Text(0), 'The value to use for the X-Mailer header.', true)
